subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: The smallest particle of an element that can take part in a chemical reaction is the

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: An atom is the smallest unit of an element that retains the chemical identity of that element.
steps: An element is made up of only one kind of atom.
The atom is the basic chemical unit of an element.
A molecule may contain two or more atoms, while an ion is a charged particle.
Therefore the correct answer is atom.
explanation.simple_explanation: The smallest particle of an element that can participate in chemical change is an atom.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: A molecule is best described as

options.C: two or more atoms chemically combined

answer_text: two or more atoms chemically combined

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: A molecule is formed when atoms join together chemically.
steps: Atoms can combine to form larger particles.
When two or more atoms are chemically joined, the resulting particle is called a molecule.
For example, H₂, O₂ and H₂O are molecules.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.final_answer: C. two or more atoms chemically combined
explanation.simple_explanation: A molecule consists of two or more atoms chemically combined.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: An ion is a particle that

options.A: has equal numbers of protons and electrons always

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: An ion is any atom or group of atoms that has gained or lost electrons and therefore carries charge.
steps: A neutral atom has equal numbers of protons and electrons.
If electrons are lost or gained, the particle becomes charged.
A charged atom or charged group of atoms is called an ion.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: An ion is a charged particle.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: Which of the following is a monoatomic ion?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: A monoatomic ion consists of a single atom carrying charge.
steps: Na⁺ is one sodium atom that has lost one electron and carries a positive charge.
H₂O and Cl₂ are neutral molecules.
NH₄⁺ is a polyatomic ion because it contains more than one atom.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Na⁺ is a monoatomic ion because it is a single charged atom.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: Which of the following is a molecule of an element?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: A molecule of an element contains atoms of the same element only.
steps: O₂ consists only of oxygen atoms.
NaCl and H₂SO₄ are compounds because they contain more than one element.
NH₄⁺ is a polyatomic ion, not a neutral molecule of an element.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: O₂ is a molecule of an element because it contains only oxygen atoms.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: The difference between an atom and an ion is that an ion

options.B: is always larger than an atom
options.C: carries charge due to electron loss or gain

answer_text: carries charge due to electron loss or gain

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: An ion differs from a neutral atom because it has gained or lost electrons and so is charged.
steps: An atom is usually electrically neutral.
If it loses electrons, it becomes a positive ion; if it gains electrons, it becomes a negative ion.
This charge is the key difference.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.final_answer: C. carries charge due to electron loss or gain
explanation.simple_explanation: An ion carries charge because electrons have been lost or gained.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: Which of the following contains a polyatomic ion?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: A polyatomic ion is a charged group of atoms acting as a unit.
steps: SO₄²⁻ is the sulphate ion and contains more than one atom.
Na₂SO₄ therefore contains a polyatomic ion.
MgO contains monoatomic ions Mg²⁺ and O²⁻, while Cl₂ and Ne do not contain ions here.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Na₂SO₄ contains the polyatomic sulphate ion, SO₄²⁻.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: Which of the following is an example of a cation?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: A cation is a positively charged ion.
steps: Cl⁻, OH⁻ and NO₃⁻ are all negatively charged ions called anions.
K⁺ carries a positive charge and is therefore a cation.
Hence option C is correct.
Cations are usually formed by loss of electrons.
explanation.simple_explanation: K⁺ is a cation because it is positively charged.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: A neutral atom becomes a negative ion when it

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: A negative charge results when the number of electrons becomes greater than the number of protons.
steps: Electrons carry negative charge.
When an atom gains electrons, it has excess negative charge.
That makes it a negative ion or anion.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: A neutral atom becomes a negative ion by gaining electrons.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: Which of the following is a compound?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: A compound contains atoms of different elements chemically combined.
steps: CO₂ contains carbon and oxygen atoms.
H₂ and O₃ each contain only one element, and Ne is a noble gas atom.
So only CO₂ is a compound.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: CO₂ is a compound because it contains atoms of different elements.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: The particle represented by NH₄⁺ is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: NH₄⁺ contains more than one atom and carries a positive charge.
steps: NH₄⁺ is made up of nitrogen and hydrogen atoms bonded together.
Because the whole group carries a positive charge, it is a cation.
Since it contains several atoms, it is polyatomic.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: NH₄⁺ is a polyatomic cation.

subtopic: Concept of Atoms, Molecules and Ions
question: Which statement is correct?

options.C: Some ions are groups of atoms carrying charge

answer_text: Some ions are groups of atoms carrying charge

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on concept of atoms, molecules and ions.
explanation.principle: Ions may be single atoms or groups of atoms.
steps: A polyatomic ion such as NO₃⁻ or SO₄²⁻ consists of a group of atoms carrying overall charge.
Therefore not all ions are single atoms.
Also, molecules are not necessarily ions, and atoms are not molecules.
So option C is the correct statement.
explanation.final_answer: C. Some ions are groups of atoms carrying charge
explanation.simple_explanation: Some ions are groups of atoms carrying an overall charge.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Dalton’s atomic theory proposed that matter is made up of tiny indivisible particles called

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Dalton introduced the idea that matter is composed of atoms.
steps: John Dalton proposed that matter is made up of tiny particles.
He called these particles atoms.
This was one of the earliest scientific atomic theories.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Dalton proposed that matter is made of atoms.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: The experiment of J. J. Thomson led to the discovery of the

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Thomson discovered the electron through cathode ray experiments.
steps: J. J. Thomson investigated cathode rays.
From these studies, he concluded that atoms contain negatively charged particles.
These particles were later called electrons.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Thomson discovered the electron.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Millikan is best known for determining the charge on the

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Millikan’s oil drop experiment measured the charge of the electron.
steps: Robert Millikan carried out the oil drop experiment.
This experiment allowed the charge on a single electron to be determined.
It was an important contribution to atomic physics.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Millikan determined the charge on the electron.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Rutherford’s alpha-particle scattering experiment showed that the atom has

options.A: a solid interior with no empty space
options.B: a small dense positively charged nucleus
options.C: electrons embedded in a positive sphere
options.D: equal masses of protons and electrons

answer_text: a small dense positively charged nucleus

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Rutherford concluded that most of the mass and positive charge of the atom are concentrated in a small nucleus.
steps: Most alpha particles passed straight through the foil, showing that the atom is mostly empty space.
A few were deflected strongly, indicating a small dense centre of positive charge.
This centre is called the nucleus.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. a small dense positively charged nucleus
explanation.simple_explanation: Rutherford showed that the atom contains a small dense positively charged nucleus.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Moseley’s work established the concept of

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Moseley showed that elements are better arranged by atomic number than by atomic mass.
steps: Henry Moseley studied X-ray frequencies of elements.
He showed that each element has a unique nuclear charge.
This led to the modern concept of atomic number.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Moseley established the concept of atomic number.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Bohr’s atomic model suggested that electrons

options.A: move randomly anywhere around the nucleus
options.B: exist in fixed energy levels around the nucleus

answer_text: exist in fixed energy levels around the nucleus

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Bohr proposed that electrons occupy specific permitted orbits or energy levels.
steps: According to Bohr, electrons do not move in just any orbit.
They occupy fixed energy levels around the nucleus.
Energy is absorbed or emitted when electrons move between these levels.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. exist in fixed energy levels around the nucleus
explanation.simple_explanation: Bohr suggested that electrons occupy fixed energy levels around the nucleus.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Which scientist is associated with the oil drop experiment?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: The oil drop experiment was carried out by Millikan.
steps: Millikan’s name is closely associated with the oil drop experiment.
This experiment measured the charge on the electron.
Therefore the scientist is Millikan.
Hence option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Millikan carried out the oil drop experiment.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: The ‘plum pudding’ model of the atom was proposed by

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Thomson proposed that electrons were embedded in a positively charged sphere.
steps: Before Rutherford’s model, Thomson suggested the atom resembled a positively charged mass containing embedded electrons.
This became known as the plum pudding model.
Therefore Thomson proposed it.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The plum pudding model was proposed by Thomson.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Which pair is correctly matched?

options.C: Moseley — arranged elements by atomic number

answer_text: Moseley — arranged elements by atomic number

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Moseley’s contribution was the establishment of atomic number as the basis of periodic arrangement.
steps: Rutherford discovered the nucleus, not electron charge.
Millikan measured electron charge, not the nucleus.
Bohr proposed quantized energy levels, not the electron.
Moseley linked element order to atomic number.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.final_answer: C. Moseley — arranged elements by atomic number
explanation.simple_explanation: Moseley is correctly matched with arranging elements by atomic number.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: The scientist whose model explained hydrogen line spectra using quantized energy levels was

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Bohr explained hydrogen spectra by assigning fixed energy levels to electrons.
steps: Bohr’s theory stated that electrons occupy definite energy levels.
When electrons jump between these levels, light of specific frequencies is emitted or absorbed.
This explained the line spectrum of hydrogen.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Bohr explained hydrogen line spectra using quantized energy levels.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Rutherford’s model differed from Thomson’s mainly because Rutherford proposed

options.B: a central nucleus in the atom

answer_text: a central nucleus in the atom

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: Rutherford introduced the idea of a tiny central nucleus, unlike Thomson’s uniform positive sphere.
steps: Thomson’s model spread positive charge throughout the atom.
Rutherford’s scattering experiments showed the positive charge is concentrated in a tiny nucleus.
This was the key difference between the two models.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. a central nucleus in the atom
explanation.simple_explanation: Rutherford differed from Thomson by proposing a central nucleus.

subtopic: Contributions of Dalton, Millikan, Rutherford, Moseley, Thomson and Bohr
question: Who discovered the electron?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on contributions of dalton, millikan, rutherford, moseley, thomson and bohr.
explanation.principle: The electron was discovered by J. J. Thomson.
steps: Through his work on cathode rays, Thomson identified a negatively charged particle in atoms.
This particle was named the electron.
Therefore the discoverer was Thomson.
Hence option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The electron was discovered by Thomson.

question: The nucleus of an atom contains

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on atomic structure.
explanation.principle: The nucleus is made up of protons and neutrons.
steps: Protons are positively charged particles found in the nucleus.
Neutrons are neutral particles also found in the nucleus.
Electrons are outside the nucleus.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons.

question: The atomic number of an element is the number of

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on atomic number and mass number.
explanation.principle: Atomic number is defined as the number of protons in the nucleus of an atom.
steps: Each element is identified by its proton number.
This proton number is called the atomic number.
For a neutral atom, it also equals the number of electrons, but the definition uses protons.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The atomic number is the number of protons in the nucleus.

question: The mass number of an atom is the total number of

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on atomic number and mass number.
explanation.principle: Mass number counts the nucleons, namely protons plus neutrons.
steps: Protons and neutrons are collectively called nucleons.
The mass number is the total number of nucleons in the nucleus.
Electrons are not included in mass number.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Mass number is the total number of protons and neutrons.

question: An atom has 11 protons, 12 neutrons and 11 electrons. Its mass number is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on atomic structure.
explanation.principle: Mass number = protons + neutrons.
steps: The number of protons is 11.
The number of neutrons is 12.
Mass number = 11 + 12 = 23.
Therefore option D is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The mass number is 23.

question: An atom with atomic number 17 and mass number 35 contains

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on atomic structure.
explanation.principle: Atomic number gives protons, and neutrons = mass number − atomic number.
steps: Atomic number 17 means 17 protons.
Mass number 35 = protons + neutrons.
So neutrons = 35 − 17 = 18.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: It contains 17 protons and 18 neutrons.

question: The electronic configuration of sodium, Na (atomic number 11), is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on electron configuration.
explanation.principle: Sodium has 11 electrons arranged as 2, 8, 1.
steps: A neutral sodium atom has 11 electrons.
The first shell holds 2, the second holds 8, and the remaining 1 goes into the third shell.
So the configuration is 2, 8, 1.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The electronic configuration of sodium is 2, 8, 1.

question: The electronic configuration of magnesium, Mg (atomic number 12), is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on electron configuration.
explanation.principle: Magnesium has 12 electrons arranged as 2, 8, 2.
steps: A neutral Mg atom has 12 electrons.
These fill the shells as 2 in the first shell, 8 in the second, and 2 in the third.
Therefore the configuration is 2, 8, 2.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The electronic configuration of magnesium is 2, 8, 2.

question: Isotopes are atoms of the same element having the same

options.A: mass number but different atomic number
options.B: atomic number but different mass number
options.C: number of neutrons and different protons
options.D: electronic configuration and different atomic numbers

answer_text: atomic number but different mass number

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on isotopes.
explanation.principle: Isotopes of an element have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons.
steps: Atoms of the same element must have the same atomic number.
If their neutron numbers differ, their mass numbers also differ.
Such atoms are called isotopes.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. atomic number but different mass number
explanation.simple_explanation: Isotopes have the same atomic number but different mass numbers.

question: Which pair represents isotopes?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on isotopes.
explanation.principle: Isotopes are atoms of the same element with different mass numbers.
steps: ¹²C and ¹⁴C are both carbon atoms, so they have the same atomic number.
Their mass numbers are different, making them isotopes.
The other pairs are different elements entirely.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: ¹²C and ¹⁴C are isotopes.

question: A neutral atom of aluminium, Al (atomic number 13), contains

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on atomic structure.
explanation.principle: A neutral atom has equal numbers of protons and electrons.
steps: Atomic number 13 means there are 13 protons.
Since the atom is neutral, it must also have 13 electrons.
Neutron number cannot be known unless the mass number is given.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: A neutral aluminium atom has 13 protons and 13 electrons.

question: The valency of an element with electronic configuration 2, 8, 7 is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on electron configuration.
explanation.principle: Such an atom needs one electron to complete its outer shell.
steps: The outermost shell contains 7 electrons.
An atom with 7 valence electrons usually needs 1 more electron to attain an octet.
Therefore its valency is 1.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The valency is 1.

subtopic: Shapes of s and p Orbitals
question: The shape of an s orbital is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on shapes of s and p orbitals.
explanation.principle: An s orbital is spherical in shape.
steps: The s orbital is the simplest type of atomic orbital.
Its electron cloud is symmetrical around the nucleus.
This gives it a spherical shape.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: An s orbital is spherical.

subtopic: Shapes of s and p Orbitals
question: The shape of a p orbital is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on shapes of s and p orbitals.
explanation.principle: A p orbital has two lobes and is commonly described as dumb-bell shaped.
steps: Unlike the spherical s orbital, a p orbital is elongated in opposite directions.
This gives it two lobes.
The usual description is dumb-bell shape.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: A p orbital is dumb-bell shaped.

question: The electronic configuration of chlorine, Cl (atomic number 17), is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on electron configuration.
explanation.principle: Chlorine has 17 electrons distributed as 2, 8, 7.
steps: A neutral chlorine atom contains 17 electrons.
These fill as 2 in the first shell, 8 in the second, and 7 in the third.
Thus its configuration is 2, 8, 7.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The electronic configuration of chlorine is 2, 8, 7.

question: An atom X is written as ²⁷₁₃X. The number of neutrons in X is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on atomic number and mass number.
explanation.principle: Neutrons = mass number − atomic number.
steps: The mass number is 27.
The atomic number is 13.
Neutrons = 27 − 13 = 14.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The number of neutrons is 14.

question: Isotopes of an element have similar

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on isotopes.
explanation.principle: Chemical properties depend mainly on electron arrangement, which isotopes of the same element share.
steps: Isotopes have the same atomic number and therefore the same electron arrangement for neutral atoms.
Because chemical behavior depends mainly on outer electrons, their chemical properties are similar.
Their physical properties may differ slightly because of mass differences.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Isotopes have similar chemical properties.

question: The modern periodic table is arranged in order of increasing

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on periodic table and periodicity.
explanation.principle: The modern periodic table is based on atomic number.
steps: Moseley showed that atomic number is the correct basis for arranging elements.
Thus the modern periodic table is organized by increasing atomic number.
This arrangement explains periodic repetition of properties.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The modern periodic table is arranged by increasing atomic number.

question: The alkali metals are found in Group

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on families of elements: alkali metals.
explanation.principle: Alkali metals belong to Group I of the periodic table.
steps: Group I elements include lithium, sodium and potassium.
These are called alkali metals.
They each have one valence electron.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Alkali metals are in Group I.

question: The halogens are found in Group

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on families of elements: halogens.
explanation.principle: Halogens are Group VII elements in the older group notation commonly used in school chemistry.
steps: Halogens include fluorine, chlorine, bromine and iodine.
In school-level notation, they are placed in Group VII.
They usually need one electron to complete the outer shell.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: The halogens are in Group VII.

question: Which of the following is a noble gas?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on families of elements: noble gases.
explanation.principle: Neon is a noble gas.
steps: Noble gases occupy the last group of the periodic table and are chemically unreactive.
Neon, Ne, is one of them.
Sodium, chlorine and magnesium are not noble gases.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Ne is a noble gas.

question: A characteristic property of transition metals is that they often

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on families of elements: transition metals.
explanation.principle: Transition metals commonly form coloured ions and compounds.
steps: Transition metals such as iron, copper and manganese often form compounds with characteristic colours.
They may also show variable oxidation states.
This is a key identifying property.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Transition metals often form coloured compounds.

question: Across a period from left to right, atomic size generally

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on periodic table and periodicity.
explanation.principle: Across a period, increasing nuclear charge pulls electrons closer, reducing atomic size.
steps: As one moves across a period, the number of protons increases.
Electrons are added to the same main energy level.
The stronger nuclear attraction pulls the electron cloud closer to the nucleus.
Therefore atomic size generally decreases.
explanation.simple_explanation: Atomic size generally decreases across a period from left to right.

question: The ionization energy of elements generally increases across a period because

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on variation of ionization energy.
explanation.principle: Greater nuclear attraction makes electrons harder to remove across a period.
steps: Across a period, nuclear charge increases.
Atomic radius generally decreases, so outer electrons are held more strongly.
As a result, more energy is needed to remove an electron.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. nuclear attraction for electrons increases
explanation.simple_explanation: Ionization energy increases across a period because electrons are held more strongly by the nucleus.

question: Electronegativity generally increases across a period from left to right because atoms

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on variation of electronegativity.
explanation.principle: Smaller atoms with greater nuclear charge attract shared electrons more strongly.
steps: Across a period, effective nuclear attraction increases.
This makes atoms better able to attract electrons in a bond.
That is the meaning of increasing electronegativity.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. attract bonding electrons more strongly
explanation.simple_explanation: Electronegativity increases across a period because atoms attract bonding electrons more strongly.

question: A cation is usually smaller than the neutral atom from which it is formed because

options.B: it loses electrons and the remaining electrons are pulled closer

answer_text: it loses electrons and the remaining electrons are pulled closer

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on variation of ionic radii.
explanation.principle: Loss of electrons reduces electron-electron repulsion and increases the pull on remaining electrons.
steps: When an atom forms a cation, it loses one or more electrons.
This may remove the outer shell or reduce repulsion between electrons.
The remaining electrons are attracted more strongly to the nucleus, making the ion smaller.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. it loses electrons and the remaining electrons are pulled closer
explanation.simple_explanation: A cation is smaller because it has lost electrons and the remaining electrons are pulled closer to the nucleus.

question: Electron affinity refers to the tendency of an atom to

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify or calculate the correct answer to this question on variation of electron affinity.
explanation.principle: Electron affinity is related to an atom’s tendency to gain an electron.
steps: When an atom accepts an electron, energy change may occur.
The ease or tendency with which an atom does this is connected with electron affinity.
Thus electron affinity concerns electron gain.
Therefore option B is correct.
